rows.definition: A capability is supported when fak has an implemented path that accepts the case without asserting how broadly it is exercised, whether every answer is right, how it compares to a peer, or whether it is production-mature.
rows.distinction: Support is the existence of the path or feature; coverage measures the space exercised or positioned, correctness proves behavior against a contract/oracle, parity compares against a named baseline, and maturity summarizes readiness.

rows.definition: Coverage is the measured fraction of a declared or discovered universe that has rows, tests, witnesses, or other positioned evidence.
rows.distinction: Coverage says how much of the space is seen; it does not by itself prove support exists for every case, correctness of behavior, peer parity, or operational maturity.

rows.definition: Correctness is the property that the implementation's behavior matches its stated contract or oracle for the case being judged.
rows.distinction: Correctness is about right behavior; support can exist before correctness is proven, coverage only says what was examined, parity is one comparative witness, and maturity adds readiness beyond the proof.

rows.definition: Parity is a same-workload comparison against a named peer, baseline, or oracle, reporting match, lead, or trailing behavior under the stated conditions.
rows.distinction: Parity is comparative; it may witness correctness when the peer is the oracle, but it is not raw support, not coverage breadth, and not the broader maturity state.

rows.definition: Maturity is the readiness level of a supported capability after considering implementation completeness, evidence breadth, correctness witnesses, parity results, guardrails, and operating posture.
rows.distinction: Maturity is the ladder over time; it is not mere support, a coverage percentage, a single correctness proof, or one parity result.
